prompt: A realistic, high-resolution drone photograph looking straight down at a modern landscaping installation during golden hour, with long, dramatic shadows enhancing the 3D depth. The landscape is divided into a zone of smooth white gravel and a zone of dark black soil. Scattered across the soil are a few stray, distinct white river stones, and scattered across the white gravel are a few stray, dark obsidian rocks. A single, continuous, non-intersecting sheet of heavy, rusted corten steel acts as a retaining wall between the two zones. Instead of a smooth, logical curve, the steel wall violently zig-zags in a chaotic, jagged pattern. It makes massive, absurd detours and sharp, narrow spikes specifically to perfectly wrap around every single misplaced white stone in the black soil, and cuts deep winding indentations to perfectly exclude every stray obsidian rock in the white gravel. The continuous steel wall is a ludicrously complex, hyper-specific maze perfectly tracing random noise rather than following the natural landscape. Absolutely no text, diagrams, labels, graphs, or floating markers.
metaphor: The steel barrier acts as a decision boundary. Its absurd, hyper-complex zig-zagging to wrap around individual misplaced stones perfectly mirrors an overfit model memorizing discrete noise rather than generalizing.
